history.question: A company is testing a new product for defects. The probability that a randomly chosen product is defective is 0.05. If a batch contains 10 products, what is the probability that exactly 2 products in the batch are defective? Assume independence between products.

A company has two machines, A and B, manufacturing widgets. Machine A produces 60% of the widgets, and Machine B produces 40%. The probability that a widget produced by Machine A is defective is 3%, while the probability that a widget produced by Machine B is defective is 7%. If a randomly selected widget is found to be defective, what is the probability that it was produced by Machine A?
